seo_title: MIT Scientists Build World's Largest Collection of Olympiad-Level…
meta_description: The MIT scientists have created a massive dataset of over 30,000 math problems from 47 countries, providing a new training ground for students and AI…
body_html:
The MIT scientists have created a massive dataset of over 30,000 math problems from 47 countries.

Comprises more than 30,000 expert-authored problems and solutions spanning 47 countries Covers 17 languages Includes both text- and image-based problems and solutions

Broad Coverage of Mathematical Perspectives

Decades of Competition Mathematics The dataset spans four decades of competition mathematics, capturing the full range of mathematical perspectives and problem-solving traditions that exist across the global math community.

Researchers at MIT’s Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL), King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST), and the company HUMAIN collaborated on the project Tracked down 1,595 PDF volumes to create the dataset

source.title: MIT scientists build the world’s largest collection of Olympiad-level math problems, and open it to everyone
source.summary: New dataset of 30,000-plus competition math problems from 47 countries gives AI researchers a harder test — and students worldwide a better training ground.
